item.title: Wireless-Powered Full-Azimuth Podded Thruster
item.description:
This project develops a wireless-powered full-azimuth podded thruster for unmanned vessels. It targets sealing reliability and maintenance challenges in conventional podded propulsion systems by transferring power wirelessly into the sealed propulsion unit.

Key responsibilities included wireless power transfer system modeling and parameter design, high-frequency wireless power converter control and integration, motor drive control, sealed mechanical structure design, 3D modeling, prototype integration, debugging, and validation.

System highlights include a 1200 W / 100 V GaN motor driver, a 5 kW integrated WPT inverter, a 5-10 cm transfer distance, 92% peak efficiency, and a sealed mechanical structure.

item.title: Active-Rectifier Wireless Motor System
item.description:
This research studies an active-rectifier wireless motor system with dual-side phase shift control. It focuses on coordinated control of the wireless power transfer stage and motor-side energy conversion for compact wireless motor drive applications.

Related paper:

item.title: An active-rectifier wireless motor system with dual-side phase shift control for phase synchronization and efficiency optimization
Magnetic-field-editable wireless power transfer system with DD 2 magnetic couplers for position detection and misalignment tolerance
Maximizing wireless power transfer efficiency at exceptional points
Optimizing dynamic performance of virtual synchronous generator based on grid-connected switching control strategy using RBFNN integrated nonlinear active disturbance rejection control approach
Power Boosting of Strongly Coupled Wireless Power Transfer Systems by Aligning 1st/3rd Harmonic Frequencies with Splitting Frequencies
Robust Maximum Power Point Tracking for Parity-Time Symmetric Wireless Power Transfer Systems in Broken/Exact Phases
Adaptive Sliding Mode Control for Electronic Throttle Systems
item.description:
This research line develops adaptive finite-time and fixed-time sliding mode control algorithms for electronic throttle control systems. The work addresses nonlinear dynamics, robustness, convergence speed, and learning-assisted compensation.

Related papers include , , , , and .

Challenge and trend on energy digitalization
Fixed-time Adaptive Sliding Mode Control for Vehicular Electronic Throttle with Actuator Saturation Using Extreme Learning Machine
Optimization of WPT-based snubber circuit via coupled-mode theory to eliminate switching oscillations

Simultaneous identification of multiple parameters in wireless power transfer systems using primary variable capacitors
CV
A novel wireless-power-transfer-based snubber circuit for suppressing high-frequency oscillation of SiC power mosfets
An All-the-Time Synchronization Technique for 2.2 kW Bidirectional Inductive Power Transfer System
Analysis of Self-Oscillation Frequency in an Inductive Power Transfer System
ELM-Based Adaptive Practical Fixed-Time Voltage Regulation in Wireless Power Transfer System
ELM-Based Fixed-Time Voltage Regulation
item.description:
This research applies an extreme-learning-machine-based adaptive practical fixed-time control method to voltage regulation in wireless power transfer systems. The work targets robust regulation under nonlinear dynamics and parameter uncertainty.

Related paper:

item.title: Frequency tracking synchronization technique for a bidirectional inductive power transfer system
Maximum efficiency tracking of wireless power transfer by using receiver-side variable capacitors
Robust Speed Control for Wireless Motor Systems with Maximum Efficiency Point Tracking
